financial.relation: Controlling Shareholder/Chairman of the Executive Board
financial.note: Holds ~24.07% stake; the Osathanugrah family remains the primary strategic driver of the firm’s long-term pivot toward premiumization.
Holds ~8.69% stake; represents significant international institutional backing, often providing a floor for stock liquidity.
Holds ~4.26% stake; fluctuates based on foreign capital inflows/outflows from the SET.

supply_chain.entity: Aluminum & Glass Suppliers (e.g., BG Container Glass)
supply_chain.note: Raw material and packaging costs account for ~50% of total costs; rising aluminum prices (up 18% YTD in 2026) directly compress gross margins.

supply_chain.entity: Sugar Producers (Mitr Phol / Thai Roong Ruang)
supply_chain.note: Core ingredient for energy drinks; performance is sensitive to domestic sugar price floors and the Thai Excise Department's sugar tax tiers.
Represents >65% of OSP's distribution network; core to the M-150 brand's dominance in provincial markets.

equity_cross_holdings.note: Holds #1 position in Myanmar's energy drink market; however, 2026 geopolitical instability in the region remains a top-tier operational risk.
The growth engine for the 'Functional Drink' segment, targeting Gen Z and health-conscious 'Silver Age' consumers.

competitors.name: Carabao Group
competitors.note: Primary rival in the Bt10-12 energy drink segment; CBG’s 28.2% market share and aggressive pricing pressure OSP's pricing power.
Competes in the functional drink and 'Beauty Drink' segments; rival for shelf space in Modern Trade channels.
Rival for premium energy drink market share and international distribution dominance.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: Directly increases COGS for high-sugar variants; OSP has aggressively reformulated M-150 to lower sugar content and mitigate tax impacts.
Impacts logistics costs and the success of OSP's 2026 expansion into the UAE and Oman markets.
As international business targets double-digit growth in 2026, a weaker THB boosts translated earnings from Vietnam, Indonesia, and China.
High-intensity manufacturing (glass bottling and personal care production) makes EBITDA sensitive to Thai energy policy.
